title: Harden #1173 instrument-failure probe: null-safe + surface (don't swallow) probe failures [Copilot]
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Copilot review on PR #1173 (fix/instrument-failure-propagation): _check_js_instrumentation_status in browser_commands.py does 'except WebDriverException: return', so if the probe execute_script throws (Selenium wraps JS errors incl. document.documentElement null), it silently returns and GetCommand is recorded ok -- a fail-OPEN path contradicting the PR's fatal-on-failure intent. Mirror gap on the extension side: js-instruments.ts swallows setAttribute failure ('documentElement unwritable; probe will see nothing'). PART 1 (this issue, no-downside, user-approved): (a) null-guard the probe JS (return null if no document.documentElement); (b) on WebDriverException, LOG a warning (don't silently return); (c) extension side: log the setAttribute-failure instead of silent swallow. STAY fail-open (log + continue) -- do NOT raise/fail-closed (deferred by user) and do NOT redesign the DOM-attribute channel (accepted limitation until full-duplex sockets). Keep existing fatal-on-failure behavior intact (test_failure_prevents_measurement must still pass). Lands on fix/instrument-failure-propagation.
